text:
Andy Murrayhas an ‘unfair advantage’ at the 2024 Olympic tennis tournament inParis, according to fellow Team GB starKatie Boulter.

Murray dramaticallypulled out of the Olympic singleson Thursday, in what is meant to be his final event in tennis before his retirement from the sport.

The 37-year-old is nowsolely focusing on the men’s doubleswithDan Evansas the British sporting icon looks to clinch the fourth Olympic medal of his career.

Boulter, who is representing Great Britain in the women’s singles, withno Emma Raducanu in Paris, has explained why Murray has an ‘unfair advantage’.

But it’s absolutely nothing to do with tennis, it’s instead to do with a quirky new hobby the British players have picked up in the French capital.

Boulter was quizzed about what it was like having Murray on the Team GB team and toldHeart Radio: ‘I mean, he is probably the pro in pin collecting, he takes it very seriously.

‘Ever since the accreditation line that we were picking up our badges, he was right on the ball and getting as many as he could. So, he does a slight, unfair advantage because a lot of people come up to him for selfies and he’s trading that for pins, which is interesting. You know, tough to keep up!

‘We all get given pins from our countries, and every other country has their own pin as well. Some have a few pins which are unique, and some are special, and some aren’t, but they trade them.

‘So, at night you’ll see people go out – we went out last night, like all of us together on our Team GB bikes – looking for pins and some people that we can trade pins with.

‘It’s insane, it is. It’s very unique and very special, but some are better than others, I will say that. I’m not sure my game’s there yet!’

Murraypaid an emotional farewellto Wimbledon this year and one of his sponsors, Castore, has urged those in SW19 to rename Church Road as ‘Murray Mile’ in his honour.

‘Andy’s legacy is unequalled,’ Tom Beahon, Castore co-founder said. ‘His desire to compete and push himself beyond his limits in order to win is inspiring.

‘It only makes sense that the SW19 site where he achieved greatness should immortalise his excellence. As an ambassador and investor, he embodies our better never stops mentality both on and off the court and we hope the public join us in celebrating his achievements with a permanent legacy.’

title: Team GB star says Andy Murray has 'unfair advantage' at Paris Olympics | Metro News
description: Andy Murray has an 'unfair advantage' at the 2024 Olympic tennis tournament in Paris, according to fellow Team GB star Katie Boulter.
